title: Cutandia philistaea
taxonomy: Cutandia philistaea is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Cutandia, which belongs to the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Cutandia is further classified under the tribe Poeae. This species is native to the Mediterranean region and is also found in other parts of Europe, Asia, and Africa. Cutandia philistaea is often found growing in dry, sandy areas and is a common component of grassland ecosystems.

intro: Cutandia philistaea, also known as Philistea grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cutandia philistaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cutandia philistaea is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
